blocks.content: Two kilometers from the First Rebel Command.
Gaold was walking through the dark forest.
With his hands behind his back, his eyes were fixed straight ahead, but they didn't take in the scenery.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Twenty years ago, he met a woman.
She was so different from him that, even though he disliked her, his curiosity made him look at her.
Human connections are truly mysterious. The heart has no substance, so you can't predict where it will flow.
Perhaps Gaold was regretting it.
Maybe he hated this reality, where all that was left was hell, because he had given everything to that woman.
But he couldn't cut off the heart he had given away, nor could he take it back.
So he walked through this hell and arrived at the center of a quiet clearing.
In the silence, where not even the sound of birds could be heard, Gaold looked up at the open sky.
Countless stars were shining.
It had been a long time since he had seen the sky.
Miro wasn't among those stars. She was somewhere connected to the ground he was standing on, alive.
Thump, thump. Thump, thump.
Calming his pounding heart, Gaold spoke into the deep forest.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Come out already. There's no need to waste time since I'm alone.
As soon as he finished speaking, 21 figures emerged from the darkness of the forest.
Cage B Team of the Tormia Magic Association.
Once, they had all been Gaold's subordinates, but few of them were familiar faces.
However, as the former head of the Magic Association, he knew well how powerful they were.
Of course, none of them could surpass Gaold in individual combat, but just as scissors beat rock, and the sea beats the wind, there are always unique magical affinities.
Also, if they were a team sent to eliminate him, they would have included irregulars.
Team leader Rose stepped forward.
Her face was marked with a dense killing intent that Gaold had never seen before.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: When did you realize?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Ha, did I need to realize? If you're targeting me, it's obvious.
It was also why Gaold had come alone.
The 21's goal was to eliminate Gaold, and they would come with the determination that it didn't matter how many of them died to achieve that goal.
If Gaold wasn't alone, they would never move and would wait for an opportunity to strike from behind.
It was something that had to be finished before the project they had prepared for 20 years could begin.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: Surround him.
At Rose's command, the 20 members intertwined like a net and teleported.
Gaold, still with his hands behind his back, only looked at Rose.
He had no feelings about avoiding the fight or not wanting to kill his former comrades.
But Cage B Team knew Gaold, and Gaold didn't know Cage B Team.
Even if he did know them, the tactical and strategic variations created by the combination of 20 different individuals were nearly infinite.
All he could do was think about what he could do.
Rustle, rustle.
Even the sound of grass brushing against each other sounded like thunder.
A few people moved behind him.
Carnivore addict Benifis drew his meat cleaver and looked at his comrades on either side.
Beam of insect magic nodded, and Reed of curse magic combined omniscience and omnipotence.
Facing the mage with the greatest physical strength in Tormia, everyone had the same thought.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: This won't take long.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hey, juniors.
At the sound of Gaold's voice, all 21 of them flinched simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Why are you taking so long to prepare? You came here to die.
Killing intent filled the mages' eyes.
As if 21 flames were burning, smoke seemed to rise from their bodies.
A ritual of burning their remaining lifespan, igniting their will.
In Gaold's absence, Sein and eight others gathered in a circle around a torch in the clearing behind the command.
Sein's expression was more relaxed than usual.
Perhaps because he was recalling memories from 20 years ago, before he became known as the ruthless mage of the Black Line.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: About the Judgment of the Twenty…
Sein began to speak.
There aren't many people in the world who know the details. But the story involving Miro, Gaold, and me is known only to the three of us.
The group waited eagerly for him to continue.
But Sein's mouth didn't open easily.
It was a burden to reveal a very small and personal secret that had occurred within the greatest event in human history.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: I loved Miro.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: And Gaold loved Miro too.
Sein looked up at the sky, just like Gaold.
Yes, it may not seem like an important story. But to us, it was everything.
This is the story.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Twenty years ago, the coldest man in the world, the most outstanding woman in the world, and…
A man who wanted to live only in obedience to God, but ended up hating, cursing, and mocking God, diving into hell in terrible sorrow.
The kindest fool in the world was attending Alpheas Magic School.
The Tormia Yore Order.
The Yore Order is a global religion that, even during humanity's dark ages, never waged a single war and only bestowed mercy.
There are no ranks among them, and they don't distinguish between commoners and nobles.
The church is inhabited only by the 'Yora,' who preach the order's doctrines. They live in poverty, relying solely on the volunteer work and donations of the believers, earning the love of all.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: 'Thank you, Yore God.'
A young man knelt under the sunlit order, holding a pendant with a small circle and cross, the symbol of Yore, and praying.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Today, I hope all the unfortunate people in the world find happiness. Make me your servant. Give me the courage to love even evil.
The young man had a white face and skin as clean as a child's.
He had a kind impression, filled with nothing but purity. This was Gaold, 20 years ago.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Are you giving thanks for advancing to the graduation class?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Yora.
Gaold, blushing with embarrassment, wiped his tears and stood up.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Graduation classes start today.
Mikael Gaold.
He was an exceptionally kind child, even among the Yore believers.
It was surprising when he said he wanted to enter a magic school.
Magic is power, and power begets violence. But Gaold wanted to learn magic for that very reason.
Even though divinity and magic have similarities, mages deny God. To understand them, he thought he had to understand magic himself.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Well, I'm off.
Gaold headed to Alpheas Magic School.
He had barely made it to the graduation class through hard work. Fierce competition awaited, but he was determined not to lose to anyone.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: 'Yore God, give me strength.'
While the entrance ceremony for the advanced class was taking place, Gaold, who was walking in the park, spotted the person he least wanted to see.
Sein, his rival since Class Seven.
But he couldn't pretend not to see him, so Gaold extended his hand for a handshake.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: From today, the real competition begins. Let's do our best.
Sein didn't even look at him.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Why should I shake hands with you? If you want to shake hands with me, bring your God here.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I've never forced my beliefs on you. Why are you always picking a fight? Your manner of speaking is really…
Another tedious argument began.
About ten minutes later.

blocks.speaker: Adrias Miro
blocks.content: Hey, you two over there!
A woman's voice called out from a distance. Sein's eyes sparkled as he turned his head.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Can't you hear me? Come over here.
As the two approached, Miro put down the box of experimental materials and wiped her sweat.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Phew, this is really heavy. You two are new students, right? Move this to my lab.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Why should we? I heard there's no seniority concept in the graduation class.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Well… you two seem to be wasting your energy. It would be nice if you could save your strength and help me.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: That's true. I'll help…

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Wait! Where is your lab…?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Please.
Miro didn't listen and ran out of the park.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Help me move this to my lab. It's dangerous, so be careful. Please.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Wait!
Gaold smacked his lips and looked at Sein.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Though they were rivals, he could sympathize with what Sein was feeling as a fellow man.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hey, are you okay?
Sein slowly pulled up his pants. Still in a daze, he turned and walked away.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: How could she pull down his underwear? She's really strange.
